gene_region.label: FCER1G transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical FCER1G genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: FCER1G encodes the Fc receptor gamma signaling chain used by IgE, IgG, and innate immune receptor complexes. This topic bundle emphasizes allergy and inflammation, with interpretation centered on IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local FCER1G bundle is oriented toward allergy and inflammation. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: FCER1G variants can alter, tag, or contextualize IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Allergy, asthma, atopy, and inflammatory disease context
Mast-cell, eosinophil, cytokine, and chemokine pathway biology
Immune signaling, barrier inflammation, and therapeutic target review
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: FCER1G methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for FCER1G.
Because FCER1G biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use FCER1G promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation.
Allergy, asthma, atopy, and inflammatory disease context
Mast-cell, eosinophil, cytokine, and chemokine pathway biology

biorender_visuals.label: FCER1G biochemical figure starter

biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a FCER1G allergy and inflammation thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_records.common_name: FCER1G allergy and inflammation pathway model
variant_records.clinical_interpretation: FCER1G immune-response variation model is curated as a FCER1G marker for Allergy, asthma, atopy, and inflammatory disease context, Mast-cell, eosinophil, cytokine, and chemokine pathway biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.

functional_effects: Can modify or tag IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation.
